rationale: Chemistry has the classic four-subdiscipline structure (organic, inorganic, physical, analytical) but the modern field needs more: I split out Catalysis, Electrochemistry, and Polymer Chemistry as their own cards because each has dedicated journals and conference circuits. Computational Chemistry deserves its own card — it's a huge community spanning DFT methods to MD simulations. I kept Biochemistry under Biology level-1 and explicitly do NOT duplicate here. The picker should feel like the table-of-contents of a chemistry conference.

subspecialties.name: Organic Chemistry
covers: synthetic methodology, total synthesis, asymmetric catalysis, C-H functionalization, mechanism, photoredox chemistry
JACS, Angew Chem, Org Lett
explicitly_excludes: natural product biosynthesis at gene level → Biology

subspecialties.name: Inorganic Chemistry
covers: coordination chemistry, organometallics, main-group, transition-metal, bioinorganic, metal-ligand bonding
Inorg Chem, Eur J Inorg Chem, Dalton Trans

subspecialties.name: Physical Chemistry
covers: spectroscopy, chemical dynamics, thermodynamics, statistical mechanics of liquids, surface science, ultrafast methods
J Phys Chem A/B/C, JCP

subspecialties.name: Analytical Chemistry
covers: mass spectrometry, NMR methodology, chromatography, sensors, analytical method development, metabolomics methods
Anal Chem, JASMS, Analyst

subspecialties.name: Catalysis
covers: heterogeneous catalysis, homogeneous catalysis, biocatalysis-enzyme engineering, electrocatalysis, photocatalysis
ACS Catal, J Catal, Catal Sci Technol

subspecialties.name: Electrochemistry
covers: batteries, fuel cells, supercapacitors, electroanalysis, corrosion electrochemistry, CO2 reduction
J Electrochem Soc, Electrochim Acta

subspecialties.name: Polymer Chemistry
covers: polymerization mechanism, controlled radical polymerization, polymer self-assembly, supramolecular chemistry, biopolymers from chem angle
Macromolecules, Polym Chem

subspecialties.name: Computational & Theoretical Chemistry
subspecialties.tagline: DFT, MD, QM/MM, ML for chemistry.
covers: DFT methodology, ab initio methods, MD simulation, QM/MM, ML for chemistry, computational mechanism
JCTC, JCC, J Chem Theory Comput
explicitly_excludes: pure ML methodology → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Medicinal Chemistry & Drug Discovery
covers: drug design, SAR studies, ADMET, fragment-based drug discovery, kinase chemistry, GPCR chemistry, PROTAC chemistry
J Med Chem, ACS Med Chem Lett

subspecialties.name: Green & Sustainable Chemistry
subspecialties.tagline: Renewable feedstocks, atom economy, circular chemistry.
covers: green chemistry methodology, renewable feedstocks, atom economy, circular chemistry, water-as-solvent, CO2 utilization
Green Chem, ChemSusChem, ACS Sustain Chem Eng

subspecialties.name: Environmental Chemistry
subspecialties.tagline: Atmospheric, aquatic, soil chemistry, pollutants, fate.
covers: atmospheric chemistry, aquatic chemistry, soil chemistry, pollutant fate and transport, PFAS chemistry
Environ Sci Technol, Atmos Chem Phys (chem portion)

subspecialties.name: Supramolecular & Nanochemistry
covers: host-guest chemistry, self-assembled monolayers, nanoparticle synthesis from chem angle, MOFs, COFs, cage molecules
Chem Sci, Nat Chem, JACS Au
explicitly_excludes: materials applications of nanomaterials → Materials Science

uncovered_concerns: Considered separating Photochemistry — folds under Physical Chemistry or Catalysis (photocatalysis) cleanly.
Considered Chemical Education as a level-2 — too small (~0.5%); folds under Social Sciences (Education).
Considered Chemoinformatics — folds under Computational Chemistry.
